supply_chain.dependencies: Large number of vehicles remarketed each year, consumer acceptance of appraisal process, experience in acquiring vehicles from dealers and auctions
supply_chain.risks: Supply of late-model used vehicles influenced by total vehicles in operation, new vehicle sales volume, and used vehicles sold through retail/wholesale channels

regulatory_risks: Vehicle dealer and sales licensing requirements across all operating states
Consumer protection laws and privacy laws compliance
Federal truth-in-lending, consumer leasing, equal credit opportunity and fair credit reporting laws
State and local motor vehicle finance, collection, repossession and installment finance laws
Oversight by Federal Trade Commission and state regulators
Supervision by Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for financing activities
Environmental laws regarding hazardous materials and wastes (motor oil, gasoline, solvents, lubricants, paints)
Underground and above-ground gasoline storage tanks regulations
Federal and state wage-hour, anti-discrimination and employment practices laws
Evolving ESG-related laws and climate-related disclosure regulations
Potential revocation or suspension of licenses for violations
Manufacturer recall regulations (lack of authorization to complete recall-related repairs)

sanction_risks: Tariffs or imposition of new tariffs impacting automotive parts, vehicles, or steel availability and pricing
